conjecture_title: Informal conjecture on tripartite off-diagonal Ramsey numbers
summary: No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture that $r(H,K_{n,n,n}^{(3)})\leq 2^{O_H(n\log n)}$ for all $H$ was found. A closely related successor paper (arXiv:2411.13812, same author group, November 2024) addresses the polynomial-vs-exponential threshold for $r(H,K_n^{(3)})$ — the non-tripartite complete 3-graph — but does not treat the tripartite $K_{n,n,n}^{(3)}$ version. The known upper bound of $2^{O_H(n^{2-\epsilon})}$ and the $2^{\Theta(n\log n)}$ threshold in constructions for $K_n^{(3)}$ remain the closest context.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is 'Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension' by Nguyen, Scott, Seymour — it proves a conjecture of Fox, Pach, and Suk about cliques/stable sets in graphs of bounded VC-dimension. It has no connection to hypergraph Ramsey numbers or $r(H,K_{n,n,n}^{(3)})$; the fuzz match is a false positive.

search_queries: hypergraph Ramsey numbers tripartite K_nnn r(H, K_nnn) 2^{n log n} bound 2024 2025
off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey Conlon Fox Gunby He Mubayi Suk Verstraete 2404.02021 follow-up
notes: The conjecture concerns $r(H,K_{n,n,n}^{(3)})$, the off-diagonal Ramsey number against the complete tripartite 3-uniform hypergraph. The companion paper arXiv:2411.13812 (Conlon et al., November 2024) characterises when $r(H,K_n^{(3)})$ is polynomial, but $K_n^{(3)}$ and $K_{n,n,n}^{(3)}$ are distinct objects and that paper does not address the tripartite setting. A 2026 paper (arXiv:2603.16069, He–Nie–Post–Verstraete) studies quasipolynomial growth for a different Ramsey function. No paper resolving the $2^{O_H(n\log n)}$ upper bound for the tripartite case was found after an exhaustive 5-call search.
